hypothesis: Repeated XR rejection can be reduced if seeded `sysopt.xr-*` cards may only enter the queue when their authoritative `gap_source_handoff` resolves inside the current worktree runtime or declared shared truth cache, so route lanes cannot act on XR evidence that exists only in an external runtime mirror.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` treated `gap_source_handoff` as a string-alignment field for every `sysopt.xr-*` card, so a synchronized falsification could seed a future XR card that still matched `GGD/gaps/routes/work-xr.geometry-gap.json.source_handoff` even when `CLAW/control-plane/runtime/handoffs/C4-20260404T131253Z-04.json` was missing in this worktree and `/Users/zer0palab/Zer0pa Website/Website-main/CLAW/control-plane/runtime/truth-cache/packets/C4-20260403T165341Z-03.json` was also missing. After the ratchet, the verifier rejects any seeded `sysopt.xr-*` card whose `gap_source_handoff` resolves in neither local runtime nor the shared truth cache, while historical kept/rejected XR cards remain replayable and the fixed scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if seeded `sysopt.xr-*` cards fail verification when their authoritative `gap_source_handoff` is missing from both the current worktree runtime and the declared shared truth cache, and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Seeded XR systems-optimizer ratchets must resolve their authoritative handoff packet in local runtime or shared truth cache before queue entry; otherwise route lanes can inherit XR work orders whose evidence only lives in an external mirror.
